text:
Once upon a time, in the quiet village of Willowbrook, there lived a wise old crow named Oliver. His feathers weren't gray yet; they shimmered a bright iridescent blue. He had seen many winters come and go but never grew old.

Every morning, before anything stirred, Oliver would flutter from his nest high upon a gnarled oak tree into the dew-laden grass. He surveyed the town below. Young sparrows tweeted excitedly while dewdrops adorned their tails. Frogs croaked rhythms that lulled insects to sleep. But Oliver knew there was more to life than chirps and hops.

His favorite spot was beyond the hedge, where wild apples hung ripe. He liked to watch tiny squirrels gather them. One day, a young squirrel named Rosie skipped up eagerly. Oliver chuckled softly. "Don't rush," he croaked. Rosie paused and listened. Oliver explained the seasons, how some apples ripened faster than others, why owls sometimes hoot mornings
